conversation.content:
Both external tools returned no directly usable amyloid-kinetics results in this environment. I'll note that and justify the structure from the established framework plus local evidence.

**Research justification (from established Cohen–Meisl–Knowles theory, since external tools returned nothing usable here):** In the nucleated-polymerization / secondary-nucleation master equation for Abeta42, the aggregation curve is a *self-accelerating* sigmoid whose **maximum growth rate occurs before the half-time**, producing a fast rise and a comparatively gentle shoulder as monomer is depleted — an asymmetry a symmetric logistic (`(plateau−c)·c`) systematically misses, causing overshoot near the plateau. A Gompertz-type multiplicative damping of the growth flux as converted mass accumulates reproduces exactly this asymmetric shoulder. This is precisely the shape-loss failure mode (`shape=0.101`) the metrics report, so damping the growth term with a bounded `exp(−c9²·c)` factor — decoupling shou
...[10105 chars total]...
nly. It is bounded in (0,1], real, overflow-free, `≈1` at the burst onset (preserving NMSE), and decays near the plateau to sharpen the shoulder — decoupling slope-profile shape from plateau location.
- **Safety net:** At `c9 = 0` the RHS is exactly the prior best (0.9351), so the fit can only refine toward lower shape loss rather than regress. One extra constant (total 10, well under 13) keeps least-squares conditioning intact. One expression, one `evaluate_expression` call, straight-line code.
